page: The Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare-metal server configured with a dedicated AMD EPYC 9354P processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 64 physical cores running at 3.25 GHz with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 960 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this system.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 351.41 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 165.96 GB/sec. Single-core CPU performance is highly competitive, scoring in the top 10% on stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance ranks in the top 25%. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a PassMark database score in the top 10% and a Redis SET throughput of 91.34 million operations per second. Additionally, the processor's AVX-512 and vector extensions enable strong performance in lightweight LLM inference tasks.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high-density DDR5 memory, this server offers high resource density and strong qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads. However, the 960 GB local SSD storage capacity is relatively modest compared to the massive 768.0 GB memory pool, which may require external storage solutions for data-heavy deployments. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, virtualization hosts, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines.
description: The Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a dedicated bare-metal server powered by an AMD EPYC 9354P processor with 64 physical cores operating at 3.25 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, delivering 12.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 960 GB local SSD. Lacking a GPU, this server relies on its high-performance x86_64 architecture and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. Benchmarks highlight top-tier memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities, alongside strong multi-core performance. This resource-dense configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, making it well-suited for in-memory databases, virtualization, and high-throughput data processing, though its local storage capacity is modest relative to its memory size.
og_description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare-metal server featuring a 64-core AMD EPYC 9354P CPU, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, optimized for high-throughput memory and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-32c-755gb-amd is a bare-metal server with a 64-core AMD EPYC CPU, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare-metal server combining sixty-four AMD EPYC cores with high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9354P processor with 64 physical cores at 3.25 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 12.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
960 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address included
Optimized for in-memory databases, virtualization, and data processing
